list.title: GDPR data-subject requests
list.subtitle: Sorted by legal deadline. Red rows are due in 7 days or less. Tavli-admin intake only in v1 — record requests received via email, postal, or verbal channels here.
list.openEmpty: No open requests. New requests typically arrive via email (legal@tavli.ro), postal mail, or verbal report; the 30-day GDPR response clock starts at receipt.

detail.heading: GDPR {kind} request
detail.cascadeEmpty: No cascade activity yet. Entries appear once the orchestrator runs.

failureBanner.title: Cascade failed
failureBanner.body: Last failure at {at}. Inspect the worker logs for the failing handler. Re-running the orchestrator is safe — handlers are individually idempotent.
failureBanner.retryConfirm: Retry the cascade? Handlers are idempotent — completed work won't be repeated.

resolveDiner.title: Resolve diner
resolveDiner.body: Paste diner UUID(s) — comma-separated for multi-org diners. The first listed becomes the DSR's primary diner_id; the cascade still resolves all phone/email matches at run time.

verifyIdentity.title: Verify identity
verifyIdentity.body: How did you verify this is the actual data subject? (E.g., phone callback, email reply, in-person, government ID review.)

approveErasure.confirm: Approve erasure? This triggers an irreversible cascade across diner/reservation/review/audit data.

reject.title: Reject request
reject.body: Why is this request being rejected? (E.g., identity could not be verified.)

extendDeadline.title: Extend deadline
extendDeadline.body: GDPR Art 12(3) allows up to 2 months extension; Tavli policy caps at 14 days. Mandatory reason.
